mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold consistently precede positive 20-day returns, with a win rate above 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative forward returns in > 50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates reflect strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading below 105.0 indicates severe supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, which depresses earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values below 105.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 65% of historical instances.
